summary: Typosquat of @solana/web3.js exfiltrates host data via postinstall
details: This package impersonates the legitimate @solana/web3.js library under a confusable scope name. package.json declares a postinstall hook ("postinstall": "node install.js") that runs install.js automatically on npm install. install.js collects host identifiers (hostname references at lines 10, 25, 27) and transmits them off-host using curl and an HTTP POST (lines 46, 103). The combination of (a) a name confusable with a top-tier Solana SDK, (b) automatic execution at install time via lifecycle hook, and (c) hostname/system-info collection shipped to an external endpoint matches an active credential/host exfiltration supply-chain attack. Installing this package will cause arbitrary host data to be sent to an attacker-controlled destination on every npm install.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
